citations.description: Ancestry check in the gate logic
test_self_reported_stale_material_rejected covers material mismatch but not non-ancestor commit or plaintext-mutation invariance
Searched 'bound commit not ancestor', 'off history', 'not_ancestor', 'mutating the record', 'plaintext mirror', 'verdict in either direction' across all tests — no matches

findings.criterion: Additional AC: `merged_log_commit` is taken from the signature-verified payload and constrained: a test asserts a bound commit that is not an ancestor of the gated history FAILS closed, and that mutating the record's plaintext `material_fingerprint`/`merged_log_commit` fields (envelope untouched) does not change the verdict in either direction.
findings.detail: No test was found that (a) binds a non-ancestor/off-history `merged_log_commit` and asserts the gate rejects it, or (b) mutates the record's plaintext `material_fingerprint`/`merged_log_commit` fields (leaving the envelope untouched) and asserts the verdict is unchanged. The implementation code for `_commit_in_gated_history` exists in `verify_opcert.py`, but the required test contracts are absent from the entire test suite.

citations.description: Only two tests present: revoked-key fails + never-revoked control; 3 sub-requirements missing
Searched 'no grandfather pin', 'unresolvable storage anchor', 'cert stored at/before the pin', 'kill-switch', 'all its certs' — no matches

findings.criterion: Additional AC: Rollback regression (the CVE-2026-44544-style case): a cert signed by a REVOKED key, freshly stored (storage anchor S after the key's `revoked_at_log_position`), binding a valid-era ancestor `merged_log_commit`, FAILS as `key_not_valid_at_era`; the same cert stored at/before the pin PASSES; a revoked key with NO grandfather pin FAILS all its certs; an unresolvable storage anchor fails closed (current-validity required).
findings.detail: Only two of the four sub-requirements are covered: (1) freshly-stored revoked-key cert FAILS (test_revoked_key_backdated_cert_fails_at_storage_anchor, exit 1) and (2) a control with key never revoked PASSES. Missing: (a) 'same cert stored at/before the pin PASSES' — the control uses a never-revoked key, not a key revoked AFTER the cert's storage anchor; (b) 'a revoked key with NO grandfather pin FAILS all its certs'; (c) 'an unresolvable storage anchor fails closed'. No tests for these sub-cases were found anywhere.

citations.description: Implementation of _add stamps added_at_log_position — no test found for this
Searched 'trusted_env_cmd', 'trusted-env add', 'rebar trusted-env', 'helper stamps', 'stamps correctly' across all tests — no matches

findings.criterion: Additional AC: `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml` era fields are tickets-branch log positions with an operator helper (`rebar trusted-env add|revoke`) that stamps the current tickets tip; a test asserts the schema parses, the helper stamps correctly, and a malformed/legacy main-SHA era field surfaces the existing located-error behavior.
findings.detail: Schema parsing (test_loader_reads_pinned_keyring) and located-error behavior for malformed/legacy fields (test_malformed_config_raises_located_error, test_legacy_git_sha_schema_raises_located_error) are covered. The 'helper stamps correctly' sub-requirement is NOT met: no test exercises `rebar trusted-env add|revoke` to assert it stamps the current tickets-branch tip position as `added_at_log_position`/`revoked_at_log_position`. The implementation exists in `trusted_env_cmd.py` but is entirely untested.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
